audioscript.text: Section 1, Louise wants to join a video library.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a conversation between Louise and the owner of the video library.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example that has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Oh, hello.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: I'd like to join the video library.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: OK, would you like to fill in the application form now?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Yes, I can do it now.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Hold on, and I'll get a form.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Now, I'll just ask you a few questions, and then I'll get you to sign at the bottom.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: What's your full name?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Louise Cynthia Jones.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Jones?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Louis's surname is Jones, so Jones has been written on the form.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully to the conversation and answer questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Oh, hello.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: I'd like to join the video library.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: OK, would you like to fill in the application form now?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Yes, I can do it now.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Hold on, and I'll get a form.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Now, I'll just ask you a few questions, and then I'll get you to sign at the bottom.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: What's your full name?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Louise Cynthia Jones.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Jones?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: OK, and what's your address?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Apartment 1, 72 Black Street, Highbridge.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Black street.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: That's just around the corner, isn't it?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Yes.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: OK, so the postcode is 2 0 8 5 right?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: And your telephone number, I need both home and work.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Home is 98356712, and work is 94561309.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Do you need any ID or anything like that?
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Uh. Yes, we need your driver's license number.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: That is, if you have one.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Yes, I know it off by heart.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: It's an easy one.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Do you need to see it?
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Uh. Yes, I'm afraid I do.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Here.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Right, thanks.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: And could you tell me your date of birth please?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: That's the most important part out of the way, but could I just ask you a few questions for a survey we're conducting?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Before the conversation continues, you have some time to look at questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: What kind of videos do you prefer to watch?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Uh. Have a look at this list.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Well, I love anything that makes me laugh.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: I just love to hear jokes and funny punch lines.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: I'm not very keen on westerns, although my father likes them.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: But I'm a real softy, so anything with a bit of a love story is good for me.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: It doesn't matter how old, not musicals though, they're too much.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Hmm, anything else?
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: I'm completely taken by documentaries of the great outdoors.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: You know the sort, animals, plants and far away places.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: I saw a wonderful one on dolphins last week.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: It was amazing.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Now, I think that's all from me, except I need you to sign here on the line.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Um. Here's a pen.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Oh, and I nearly forgot the membership fee, $25 refundable if you leave the library for any reason.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: There you are.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Uh. And do I sign here?
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Yes, that's it.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: You can borrow videos now if you like, but your card won't be ready until next week.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: You can come and pick it up when you bring your first videos back.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: That is if you want to take some now.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: Yes, I'd like to.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: I'll have a look around.
audioscript.speaker: LOUISE

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: MR MAX

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: An Expedition across Morocco's Attora Mountains

audioscript.text: Section 2, you will hear a radio interview about an expedition across the Attora Mountains.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 15.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully to the interview, and answer questions 11 to 15.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: A dream came true in 1995, when over 96 days of the spring and summer, an expedition of four men undertook what they believe to have been the first and only complete end to end crossing of Morocco's Attora mountains.
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: I talked to Charles Owen, the leader of the expedition group, about the trip.
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: Charles, how much planning went on beforehand?
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: Um. Well, as you know, I run these walking trips across the mountains for tourists.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: And over the years, I've collected maps and other data to prepare what I call a 'route book' for this trip.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: And this book basically shows the route across the mountains that we took.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Hmm, you actually broke records while you were out there, didn't you?
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: Mmm. Yes, it was 900 miles in total, and we managed to climb 32 peaks that were over 3,000 meters high, including Toubkal.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Which is of course the highest in North Africa.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Yes, uh, we weren't actually out to make a name for ourselves.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: It just happened really.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Ha ha ha.
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: What was the weather like?
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: Oh.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: It got us right from day one, and we were pretty taken aback really to find that it rained on quite a number of days, and so we were forced to start replanning our route almost from the outset.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: One of the obvious problems is the heavy snow which blocks the mountain passes.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: So you have to make considerable detours.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: When we were on the way to Imilchil, for example, the snow forced us into a northern bypass which was new to us.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: But anyway, either way we would have been rewarded, because well, we fell upon amazing high meadows, uh, huge gorges and wonderful snowcapped mountains.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: The scenery was as fine as any we saw on the trip, and that was how it was every time.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Having to take another pass was never a disappointment.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: You now have some time to look at questions 16 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen to the rest of the interview, and answer questions 16 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: It was in many ways a social trip, wasn't it?
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: Yes, yes...
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Uh. We'd arranged to meet up with friends at various points on the journey.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: I mean this was actually one of the purposes of the trip.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: And we managed to keep all these dates, which well, it's amazing really considering the detours we made.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Um. An old friend acted as a sort of transport organizer for everyone, and the Hotel Ali in Marrakech was a good social base.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: I'd really recommend it, although I can't remember who runs it.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Anyway, groups of friends actually joined us for three week stints, and others just linked up with us.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Some whom we hadn't met before the trip at all tagged on for short bursts, people from the area who just came along for the ride, but outside the major visitor areas like Toubkal.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: We only met one other group of travelers like ourselves in the whole 96 days.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Were there any bad moments?
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: Um. Well, we took two, I must say long suffering donkeys with us to help transport water and tents and things.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: I suppose if we were to do it all again, we'd probably hire donkeys along the way.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Taza and Tamri as we called them.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: After the last places in the trip, well, they made quite a unique journey between them, and well, but it was continuously demanding for them, on both the really high summits, they took diversions that were quite out of character.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: And I can only assume that it must have been due to tiredness.
audioscript.speaker: CHARLES

audioscript.text: Well, thank you.
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: And Charles has put together a video about this journey, and continues to lead groups to the Attora mountains, so if you want further information.
audioscript.speaker: INTERVIEWER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: What took the group by surprise?
How did Charles feel about having to change routes?
options: He was irritated by the diversion.
It made no difference to his enjoyment.

body.title: What does Charles say about his friends?
options: He met them at one stage on the trip.
They kept all their meeting arrangements.
One of them helped arrange the transport.
One of them owned the hotel they stayed in.
Some of them travelled with him.
Only one group lasted the 96 days.

body.title: What does Charles say about the donkeys?
options: He rode them when he was tired.

test_text.title: University Life: Starting a New Term

audioscript.text: Section 3, you will hear two new students, Jane and Tim, talking about their university studies.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully to the discussion, and answer questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Hi, Tim.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Jane.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: How are you?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I've been wondering when I'd run into you.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Have you been here long?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: I arrived yesterday, uh, on Sunday.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: How about you?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I got here a few days ago, on Saturday.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Um. No, wait a minute.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: What's today?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Uh. Sorry, Friday, not Saturday.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: But we didn't have to be here till today.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Yes, I know, but I wanted to get my things moved into my room, and just take a look around.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: So, did you decide to do English in the end?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: No, I changed my mind and opted for history instead.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Oh, and you're doing biology, if I remember correctly.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Yes, although to start with, I couldn't decide between that and geography.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: How much reading have you got?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I was given an amazingly long list of books to read.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: See?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Wow, it does look pretty long.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Well, I counted 57, I could hardly believe it.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: What's your list like?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Well, it's not as long as yours, but it's still pretty big.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: There are 43. I don't know how I'm gonna get through them all.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Well, you don't have to read them all this week.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: You just have to stay ahead of the lectures and seminars.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Hmm. Have you got your class schedule yet?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Yep. It came with the reading list.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: When's your first lecture?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Tuesday.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: How about you?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: The day after, it's my busiest day.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: I've got two lectures in the morning and one in the afternoon.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Before the discussion continues, you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: It's gonna be different from school, isn't it?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Yeah, particularly the lectures.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Hmm. Have you got any special strategy for listening to lectures?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Well, I'm going to use a cassette recorder and record them all.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: What!
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Are you allowed to?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Sure, lots of people do it nowadays.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: It means you can listen to the lectures all over again later and make really good notes.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: I couldn't do that.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I like to take notes as I'm listening.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I usually find I get all the important points.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Reading is different, of course.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: My approach is to skim the book first to see what's important and what isn't.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: It saves hours of time.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: But what if you miss something?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: You don't mean you're going to read every word, do you?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Well, that's what I usually do.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Well, that's up to you, but I think you're crazy.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: What's your first lecture on anyway?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Oh, it's a lecture on the French Revolution.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: The French Revolution, how boring.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: It's not boring at all.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: It was an amazing period of history.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: It changed everything in Europe.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: So what's your first lecture about?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: It's about animal behavior.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: It sounds really interesting.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Look, I was on my way to the library.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I'm going to get some of these books out and start reading for the first essay I've got to write.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: And what have you got to write about?
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Well, you'll never believe it.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I think our professor must have a sense of humor.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: He's given us the title, "Why study history?"
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: That's a good one.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: When you find the answer, let me know.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: I'm gonna enjoy writing it.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Have you been given any writing assignments yet?
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: Yes, I've got to write about animal language.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Hmm, that sounds a challenge.
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: I suppose you'll be off to the zoo to do field research...
audioscript.speaker: TIM

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: Physical Education Training Courses and Careers

audioscript.text: Section 4, you will hear a lecture being given to school leavers on the jobs people can get in sport, and the training programs available to prepare for those jobs.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully to the lecture, and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Welcome to Further Education Information Week.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: This is the Physical Education faculty session, and I'm the head of the faculty.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: During the course of this morning, we hope to give you a clear idea of what we offer in our training programs, and we will look at the types of courses.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: And the entry requirements if any for those courses.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Uh. Some of these courses are open to school leavers, but for some, you need previous qualifications or relevant successful employment.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: So firstly, the Physical Fitness Instructor's course is offered as a 6 month certificate course, which includes an important component of personal fitness.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: But there are no specific entry requirements. For sports administrators, we provide a 4 month certificate course, but you should be aware that this is designed for those who are in employment.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: This employment must be current and related to sports administration.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: For the Sports Psychologist course, we offer a one year diploma course, but this diploma course is available only to those who already hold a degree in psychology.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: So you need to make sure you have that before you apply to do this course.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Now, for Physical Education teachers we offer a four year degree in education.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: This degree course is designed for preparing students to teach in primary and secondary schools.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: And needs no prior qualifications, as it is entered directly by school leavers.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: And lastly, for the Recreation Officer's course, we offer a 6 month certificate.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Entry to this course normally includes applicants of a wide range of ages and experiences.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: But we do not insist on any prerequisites for this course.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Remember that this is a vocational training institute.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: We train you so that you can take up a particular kind of job.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: So, it is important that you know the main roles of the jobs, what the work is like, and what kind of qualities you need to succeed at them.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: A physical fitness instructor works in health and fitness centers, preparing individual programs for ordinary members of the public.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Physical fitness instructors prepare routines of exercises to suit the individual client's age and level of fitness.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Sports administrators run clubs and sporting associations.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Their duties include such things as booking playing fields with local councils.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: And organizing the schedule of games or events for the club, so they need good organizational skills.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Sports psychologists spend time with professional athletes, helping them approach competition with a positive mental attitude to enable them to achieve their personal best.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: They do this by improving motivation and concentration or assisting with stress management.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Physical education or PE teachers instruct young students in how to exercise, play sport, and do other recreational activities correctly and safely.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: PE teachers help the development of coordination, balance, posture and flexibility with things like simple catching and throwing skills.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: They are not expected to be experts in all sports, but must be able to show students the basic techniques involved in a wide range of activities.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Recreation Officers often find themselves working for local government authorities and local groups.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: Their aim is to raise people's awareness of healthy lifestyles.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: And improve general fitness through arranging recreational activities for groups of all ages from the very young to the elderly.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: There are many other job opportunities which our graduates can look forward to.
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: If you're interested in any of these...
audioscript.speaker: LECTURER

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: LECTURER

utterances.text: If you're interested in any of these...
utterances.speaker: LECTURER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items: Type of course: duration and level
Job: Physical Fitness Instructor → Main role: <input>
Job: Physical Education Teacher  → Main role: <input>
body.title: Job → Job's main role

answer_key.correct_answer: no prior qualifications | no qualifications | none
